comment: 『玉結び』Phase 0 アセット生成計画。ミトは方向別8コマ×high-res(443pxセルraw)で生成し、right は left の水平反転。reference でキャラ一貫性を固定する。
common_avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI
footer: Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.
mito_block: the hero Mito: an androgynous 15-year-old wanderer with ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape, a vermilion braided-cord hair ornament, and calm amber eyes. Outfit (bright fantasy-Japanese traveler, NOT samurai, NOT ninja): white tube-sleeved top, a short indigo-blue haori jacket with small vermilion braided-cord knots on the sleeves and hem, loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties, light travel sandals, a vermilion braided cord worn as a belt-sash with a small tassel, and a necklace with three pale-green magatama beads. At his left hip hangs a sheathed short ritual sword in a WHITE scabbard wrapped with a thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers - it reads as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana.

assets.name: mito_walk_down
assets.prompt: Original high-resolution pixel-art PLAYER WALK CYCLE sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing DOWN (toward the viewer, face visible). {MITO} CRITICAL PROPORTION RULE: the proportions of the attached reference character EXACTLY: about 2.4 heads tall, sturdy grounded build with a solid chest, thick limbs and a broad stable stance - NOT slim, NOT lanky, NOT taller than the reference. Every frame keeps EXACTLY the same head size, the same total height and the same sturdy body width. SWORD RULE: the white katana scabbard stays sheathed at his LEFT hip and is drawn in the SAME steady position, angle and length relative to the body in EVERY frame - it must not drift left or right, must not rotate, must not lengthen or shorten, must not float away from the hip, and is NEVER held in the hand; only the body's natural walk motion moves. 8-frame WALK CYCLE with proper alternating footsteps: frame1 = CONTACT A (his LEFT foot planted forward, right foot back, arms counter-swing), frame2 = weight drop (body 2-3px lower), frame3 = PASSING (feet together under the body, body at mid height), frame4 = push-off (body 2-3px higher), frame5 = CONTACT B (his RIGHT foot planted forward, left foot back - the MIRROR step of frame1), frame6 = weight drop, frame7 = PASSING (feet together), frame8 = push-off. The forward foot MUST alternate: left foot leads in frame1, right foot leads in frame5. Arms swing opposite to the legs each step. Both feet clearly visible and readable against the ground. The haori hem and the vermilion cord tassels sway naturally with each step. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet; every drawn shape belongs to the single character inside its own cell. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, centered with clear empty margin on all four sides, feet on the same ground line in every cell, head at the same height in every cell. TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right. No grid lines, no text.

assets.name: mito_idle_down
assets.prompt: Original high-resolution pixel-art PLAYER IDLE (breathing) sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing DOWN (toward the viewer, face visible). {MITO} CRITICAL PROPORTION RULE: the proportions and body width of the attached reference (his own walking sheet) EXACTLY - same head-to-body ratio, same slim-but-sturdy balance, same total height. NOT wider, NOT stockier than the reference, EXACTLY the same head size, total height and body width in every frame. STANCE RULE: standing relaxed and natural with the feet planted a clear SHOULDER-WIDTH apart - there is a visible gap of empty ground between the two ankles; do NOT draw the legs or feet touching or pressed together, and do NOT draw a wide horse stance either; weight evenly on both feet, legs nearly vertical and relaxed, and the feet NEVER move between frames. SWORD RULE: the white katana scabbard stays sheathed at his LEFT hip in the EXACT same position, angle and length in every single frame (it must not drift, rotate, lengthen or change). 8-frame subtle BREATHING LOOP: frame1 = neutral stance, frame2 = slow inhale, chest and shoulders rise 1-2px, frame3 = inhale peak, head lifts 1px, frame4 = breath held, cord tassels drift, frame5 = exhale begins, shoulders settle, frame6 = lowest relaxed point, body 1-2px lower than neutral, frame7 = calm blink (eyes closed) while the body returns toward neutral, frame8 = eyes open, back to the neutral stance so the loop repeats seamlessly. The silhouette change between frames is SMALL and smooth - this is a quiet idle, not an action. The haori hem, the vermilion cord tassels and the small paper streamers on the sword sway very subtly throughout. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet; every drawn shape belongs to the single character inside its own cell. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, centered with clear empty margin on all four sides, feet on the same ground line in every cell, head at the same height in every cell. TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right. No grid lines, no text.

assets.name: mito_atk_down
assets.prompt: Original high-resolution pixel-art PLAYER SWORD ATTACK sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing DOWN (toward the viewer). {MITO} He draws and swings his short ritual sword: a straight bright blade with a WHITE hilt wrapped with a thin sacred rope and small white paper streamers that flutter with the motion. NOT wider, NOT stockier than the reference, EXACTLY the same head size and body size in every frame; the character body is drawn at about 70 percent of the cell height so the fully extended sword always fits INSIDE the cell with margin. 8-frame VERTICAL OVERHEAD CUT staying FRONT-FACING: his face, chest and hips keep facing the viewer squarely in EVERY frame - he never turns sideways, never swings horizontally. frame1 = WINDUP start, both hands grip the hilt in front of his chest, frame2 = WINDUP peak, the blade raised STRAIGHT UP above his head, vertical, frame3 = STRIKE, the blade chopping straight DOWN in front of his torso, frame4 = STRIKE end, arms fully extended downward-forward, the blade tip pointing at the ground in front of his feet - the decisive key pose, frame5 = FOLLOW-THROUGH, the blade low in front of him, shoulders dropped, frame6 = FOLLOW-THROUGH settles, frame7 = RECOVERY, he lifts the blade back up toward a centered two-hand guard, frame8 = centered front-facing guard stance (loops cleanly into frame1). NO motion trail, NO swoosh, NO afterimage, NO energy effect, NO glow: draw ONLY the solid body and the solid sword in every frame. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet; every drawn shape belongs to the single character inside its own cell. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, feet on the same ground line in every cell. assets.name: mito_atk_up
assets.prompt: Original high-resolution pixel-art PLAYER SWORD ATTACK sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing UP (PURE BACK VIEW: we see only his BACK - the back of his head with the small low ponytail, the back of the indigo haori, and both shoulders and arms. NO face, NO chest, the torso never twists to a side or front view). {MITO} He holds his short ritual sword with BOTH HANDS together on the hilt, the blade pointing straight AWAY from the viewer (into the screen / toward the top of the cell). CRITICAL PROPORTION RULE: the proportions and body width of the attached reference (his own walking sheet) EXACTLY - same head-to-body ratio, same total height; body at about 70 percent of cell height. KEEP THE POSE SIMPLE AND CLEAN: this is a back-view strike where fine detail is barely visible, so the only goal is that it clearly reads as a natural TWO-HANDED forward swing with NO awkwardness. 8-frame simple two-handed forward chop, back always to the viewer: frame1 = both hands hold the hilt centered in front of him, blade pointing away; frame2 = small windup, both arms draw back slightly (blade still points away); frame3 = both arms push the sword FORWARD away from the viewer; frame4 = both arms extended forward, blade pointing away along the ground ahead - the key pose; frame5 = begin settling; frame6 = settle; frame7 = return toward the centered two-hand hold; frame8 = centered two-hand hold (loops). BOTH hands stay together on the hilt in EVERY frame and both arms move together symmetrically and stay close to the body - NEVER one hand, NEVER an idle hand left hanging at the side, NEVER arms pointing in opposite directions. Do NOT raise the blade high overhead, do NOT draw an elaborate wind-up, do NOT show the blade edge prominently - keep it a small, clean, natural back-facing two-handed forward strike. NO motion trail, NO swoosh, NO afterimage, NO energy effect, NO glow: draw ONLY the solid body and the solid sword in every frame. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, feet on the same ground line in every cell. TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right. No grid lines, no text.

assets.name: bg_satoyama_sky
assets.prompt: Wide pixel-art parallax BACKGROUND for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG field: a luminous clear morning sky over a peaceful satoyama countryside horizon. Soft cumulus clouds, gentle distant green hills with terraced rice paddies, faint atmospheric haze near the horizon line. Bright, open, hopeful mood with rich saturated colors - spring sunlight. The horizon line sits in the lower third; the upper two thirds are sky and clouds. NO characters, NO buildings in the foreground, no text. Crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art, fine readable pixel clusters, painterly SNES-era sky gradients.

assets.name: bg_takamagahara_far
assets.prompt: Distant skyline SILHOUETTE band for a far parallax layer: a faint, pale, white-gold floating sky-city resting on top of a sea of clouds, seen from very far away near the horizon. Elegant shrine-like towers, thin spires and a great torii gate shape, all rendered as a soft bright silhouette with minimal detail, slightly luminous against the empty space. The silhouette band occupies the vertical middle of the image; everything that is not the city silhouette and its cloud base must be flat solid #FF00FF magenta so it can be chroma-keyed. Wide horizontal composition, no text. Crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art.

assets.name: prop_torii
assets.prompt: A single large wooden TORII GATE as one coherent pixel-art object for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG field, front view. Vermilion-lacquered round pillars with a gentle weathered texture, a dark tiled top lintel with slightly upturned ends, a thick braided sacred rope (shimenawa) with white zigzag paper streamers (shide) hanging between the pillars, small stone bases, a soft contact shadow on the ground. Bright, welcoming, sacred but cheerful. The gate is drawn LARGE filling most of the canvas with a small margin. NO characters, no text. Crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art, fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light.

assets.name: enemy_kitsunebi
assets.prompt: Original pixel-art ENEMY sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG: a kitsunebi fox-fire spirit tainted by impurity - a floating ball of pale blue-white spectral flame with two small fox-ear flame tips, a pair of simple glowing amber eyes, and thin wisps of dark purple haze (the impurity) clinging to its lower half. Cute-eerie but NOT scary, readable silhouette. 4-frame hover loop: frame1 = neutral hover, frame2 = rises slightly, flame tips lean right, frame3 = highest point, flame flickers taller, frame4 = settles down, flame tips lean left (loops to frame1). Same identity and scale every frame, legless floating creature with squash-and-stretch. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: one row of 4 equal cells, one creature per cell, centered with clear margin. No grid lines, no text.

assets.name: tile_grass_wa
assets.prompt: Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette: DEEP rich saturated greens (early-summer rice-field banks), clear blue-green water, vermilion and white accents, soft warm sunlight. NOT dry, NOT yellow-brown, NOT a western prairie or wasteland. Top-down ground TILESET of a lush Japanese SASA bamboo-grass meadow: fine SHARP POINTED narrow blades of grass in the sasa / susuki style, slender lanceolate leaves with crisp pointed tips that stand upright and lean in clusters, dense fresh deep-green growth with subtle tone variation, a few tiny sparse moss patches between the blades. ABSOLUTELY NO round clover leaves, NO rounded shamrock or trefoil foliage, NO broad rounded leaves, NO flat western lawn - every blade must read as slender and sharply pointed Japanese grass. Uniform brightness (ground luminance above 55 percent), no hard shadows. 16 tile variations in a 4x4 grid, every cell a seamless interchangeable grass tile completely filled edge to edge, no borders between cells, no text.

assets.name: tile_path_wa
assets.prompt: Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette: DEEP rich saturated greens (early-summer rice-field banks), clear blue-green water, vermilion and white accents, soft warm sunlight. NOT dry, NOT yellow-brown, NOT a western prairie or wasteland. STRICT 4x4 TILESET LAYOUT: row1 = four full CENTER variations, row2 = edge tiles with the neighbour terrain on the North / South / West / East side, row3 = outer corner tiles NW / NE / SW / SE, row4 = four accent center variations. Each cell completely filled edge to edge, tiles seamlessly tileable, no borders or gaps between cells, no text.

STRICT 4x4 TILESET LAYOUT: row1 = four full CENTER variations, row2 = edge tiles with the neighbour terrain on the North / South / West / East side, row3 = outer corner tiles NW / NE / SW / SE, row4 = four accent center variations. assets.name: tile_water_edge_wa
assets.prompt: Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette: clear blue-green water, deep matcha-green banks, soft warm sunlight. Top-down transition TILESET of a calm village pond meeting soft grassy banks. SMOOTH, GENTLE shores: the water blends into the bank through a soft gradient (clear water -> pale shallows -> wet mossy margin -> grass) with NO hard outline, NO harsh rock wall and NO sharp line at the waterline - the edge must look soft and gradual. Calm water with fine soft ripples and a faint sky reflection, smoothly shaded with no harsh banding. Only a few small smooth mossy pebbles scattered gently, never a hard rock border. STRICT 4x4 TILESET LAYOUT: row1 = four full CENTER water variations (pure rippling water), row2 = edge tiles with the grassy bank softly fading in on the North / South / West / East side, row3 = outer corner tiles NW / NE / SW / SE with a soft rounded shoreline, row4 = four accent center variations. Each cell completely filled edge to edge, tiles seamlessly tileable, the shoreline gradients aligning so neighbouring tiles blend smoothly, no hard borders or gaps between cells, no text.

assets.name: tile_detail_wa
assets.prompt: Sparse decorative OVERLAY tile set for deep-green Japanese sasa-grass ground, top-down: 16 cells in a 4x4 grid, each cell mostly EMPTY solid #FF00FF magenta with ONE small Japanese-countryside decoration near the center - a small tuft of sharp pointed sasa bamboo-grass blades, a slender clump of pointed grass, soft green moss on a small grey stone, a sprig of violet tsuyukusa dayflowers, a little pink nadeshiko, a few fallen pale-pink cherry petals, a couple of smooth river pebbles, a tiny white wood-sorrel cluster. Bright mythical-Japan palette, delicate and sparse. Pointed slender grass, NOT round clover, NOT dandelions. No text.

assets.name: obj_suisha
assets.prompt: Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette: deep matcha and moss greens, clear blue-green water, vermilion and white accents, soft warm sunlight. ONE Japanese watermill hut (suisha-goya) as a single coherent pixel-art object, drawn in a STRICT FLAT FRONT ELEVATION view: the hut faces the camera perfectly straight-on (the same flat front-facing orientation as a torii gate), NO perspective, NO three-quarter angle, NO side walls or roof sides visible. The character body is drawn at about 70 percent of the cell height so the fully extended sword always fits INSIDE the cell with margin. 8-frame IAI DRAW-CUT, one continuous motion: frame1 = IAI STANCE: knees slightly bent, body lowered and coiled, LEFT hand gripping the white scabbard at his hip, RIGHT hand resting on the hilt, the blade STILL SHEATHED, eyes focused, frame2 = deeper crouch, maximum tension, still sheathed (frames 1-2 are the charge loop), frame3 = THE DRAW BEGINS: the blade halfway out of the scabbard, body starting to twist, frame4 = BATTOU: the blade fully drawn in one lightning rising cut in FRONT of him toward the viewer, arms extended forward-downward, blade arcing up-forward - the decisive key pose, frame5 = FOLLOW-THROUGH: the swing decelerates past his body, paper streamers on the hilt trailing, frame6 = settling, weight forward, blade low, frame7 = NOTO: he guides the blade back toward the scabbard mouth, frame8 = sheathed again, calm stance (loops cleanly to frame1). NO motion trail, NO swoosh, NO afterimage, NO energy effect: draw ONLY the solid body, scabbard and sword. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, feet on the same ground line in every cell. assets.name: ka_path
assets.prompt: Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, soft warm GOLDEN-HOUR light, hand-painted Ghibli-like look. A top-down winding MOUNTAIN PATH (山道) autotile — warm earthen dirt trail with scattered flat pale stepping stones and small pebbles, soft mossy green edges, gently bordered by grass. Painterly soft, NOT flat chunky. STRICT 4x4 layout, 16 tiles, each cell EXACTLY 128x128 px, filled edge to edge, FULLY OPAQUE, seamlessly tileable. Row1 = 4 path INTERIOR variations (earth + a few flat stones, walkable). Row2 = path-to-grass EDGE in order N,S,W,E (dirt path fading to grass on that one side). Row3 = OUTER CORNERS NW,NE,SW,SE. Row4 = accents: a flat stepping stone, a mossy rock, a grass tuft, scattered fallen leaves. Soft smooth blends.

assets.name: ka_river
assets.prompt: Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, soft warm GOLDEN-HOUR light, hand-painted Ghibli-like look. A top-down flowing CLEAR MOUNTAIN RIVER water autotile — fresh blue-green flowing water with gentle white ripples and soft foam, reflecting warm sky, a pebbly stream-bed faintly visible. Painterly, calm and beautiful. STRICT 4x4 layout, 16 tiles, each cell EXACTLY 128x128 px, filled edge to edge, FULLY OPAQUE, seamlessly tileable. Row1 = 4 open flowing-WATER interior variations (gentle current ripples). Row2 = water-to-bank EDGE N,S,W,E (water meeting a pebbly grassy bank on that side). Row3 = OUTER CORNERS NW,NE,SW,SE of the bank. Row4 = accents: a wet boulder, a foam swirl, a reed clump, a mossy stepping stone. Water reads as clean fresh stream, NOT flat blue.

assets.name: ka_forest
assets.prompt: Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, soft warm GOLDEN-HOUR light, hand-painted Ghibli-like look. A top-down lush FOREST CANOPY autotile (鎮守の森 sacred grove) — dense rounded treetops of deep matcha and emerald green with soft dappled sunlight highlights and cool shadow between crowns, painterly and soft. STRICT 4x4 layout, 16 tiles, each cell EXACTLY 128x128 px, filled edge to edge, FULLY OPAQUE, seamlessly tileable. Row1 = 4 dense CANOPY interior variations (rounded treetops, no edge). Row2 = canopy-to-grass EDGE N,S,W,E (forest crowns meeting open grass on that side). Row3 = OUTER CORNERS NW,NE,SW,SE. Row4 = accents: a tall dark pine crown, a small clearing gap, a mossy boulder under trees, a leafy bush. Soft painterly, readable individual treetops.
